shownotes: KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist is likely incomplete. The band scored the festival slot after winning the Triple J Unearthed competition. Black Tooth and Willoughby's Beach were reported to have been performed (see link to Pastoral Times review below), but no recordings are available. If anyone has any further information on what was played at this show or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

This show was a live broadcast for FBi Radio Sydney. The performance began at 1pm that afternoon as The Murlocs had a show that night. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist is likely incomplete. Willoughby's Beach has been confirmed by audio/video recording; Lunch Meat and Black Tooth are shown on the setlist in the Willoughby's Beach video, along with a song named 'Jam'. The Flightless soundcloud upload of Sea of Trees has the name 'Jam' in the link, compared to the other uploads which have the name of the song in the title, so this song is assumed to be Sea of Trees. If anyone has any further information on what else was played at this show or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

Sea of Trees and Uh Oh, I Called Mum were introduced as new songs. Lunch Meat was dedicated to John Murphy. Sea Of Trees was titled “Jam” on the setlist and contained alternate lyrics. Surf Rider (The Lively Ones) was teased before Black Tooth. Footy Footy was introduced as a song about soccer. High Hopes Low was titled “Oh Sees Rip” on the setlist. KGLW.net Staff Notes: Thank you to Kieran Gilfeather and Frazer Bull-Clark for sharing their complete recording of this show with us.

This show was part of Converse's Acts of Disruption concert series, an "initiative designed to disrupt the traditional live music experience by inviting some of Australia's most exciting emerging bands to play at unique locations, chosen by them." The show was held at the vacated Rozelle Hospital. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist is likely incomplete. Bloody Ripper and Black Tooth have been confirmed by audio/video recording. If anyone has any further information on what else was played at this show or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

This show was an Industry Only event the night before the grand reopening of The Ding Dong Lounge. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist is likely incomplete. Dead-Beat has been confirmed by audio/video recording. If anyone has any further information on what else was played at this show or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

This show is a solo performance by Stu and features the first confirmed performance of It's Got Old. Stu opened for Sam Cooper (Sambrose Automobile) & Band. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist is likely incomplete. It's Got Old has been confirmed by audio/video recording. If anyone has any further information on what else was played at this show or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

jamchart_notes: Only time it was performed with Broderick and was the performance that inspired the Eyes Like The Sky album
shownotes: Sam Cherry's Last Shot featured vocals by Broderick Smith. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist is likely incomplete. Sam Cherry's Last Shot has been confirmed by audio/video recording. If anyone has any further information on what else was played at this show or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

King Gizzard is believed to have played one of the last shows at Godzilla Bar in Naarm (Melbourne), possibly even the last one. Video of the performance is documented in the attached The Final Godzilla video (with the Dead-Beat single used as the soundtrack). KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist is likely incomplete. Muckraker and High Hopes Low have been confirmed by audio/video recording. If anyone has any further information on what else was played at this show or, ideally, a complete recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist is likely incomplete. A cover song by the Reatards was reported to be played by a blog called Small Town Disease. 'I Gotta Rock n Roll' is the only Reatards song King Gizz is known to cover. The review also mentions a song with theremin, so it is likely that Muckraker, Dead-Beat, Crookedile, or Uh Oh I Called Mum could have been performed. Which song of those that was performed cannot be confirmed without any further audio/video evidence. If anyone has any additional information on what was played at this show or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.
